title: Quiz: Learning Rate Schedules
description: Test your understanding of learning rate strategies

questions.question: What happens when the learning rate is too high?
questions.explanation: When the learning rate is too high, gradient steps overshoot the optimum, causing oscillation around it or complete divergence where the loss increases unboundedly.

questions.question: What is the purpose of learning rate warmup?

options.text: To increase the final learning rate
To gradually increase LR at start for stable training
To eliminate the need for gradients

questions.explanation: Warmup gradually increases the learning rate from a small value at the start, allowing adaptive optimizers to calibrate and preventing early divergence when initial gradients may be unreliable.

questions.question: How does cosine annealing change the learning rate?

options.text: Follows a cosine curve from max to min LR

questions.explanation: Cosine annealing smoothly decreases the learning rate following a cosine curve from the initial LR to a minimum LR, providing gradual decay without sudden drops.

questions.question: For noisy quantum gradients, a lower learning rate is generally safer.
questions.explanation: True. Lower learning rates reduce the amplification of gradient noise, leading to more stable updates. However, this trades off with slower convergence.

questions.question: What does 'reduce on plateau' scheduling do?

options.text: Decreases LR when loss stops improving
Resets LR to initial value periodically

questions.explanation: Reduce on plateau monitors the loss, and when improvement stalls for a number of steps (patience), it reduces the learning rate to allow finer optimization around the current point.

questions.question: For VQA with 100 shots, what learning rate range is typically suggested?
questions.explanation: With only 100 shots, gradient estimates are noisy. A learning rate of 0.01-0.05 balances making progress while not amplifying noise too much. Higher shots allow higher LR.

questions.question: What is layer-wise learning rate scheduling?

options.text: Using different LR for different training epochs
Using different LR for parameters in different circuit layers
Training one layer at a time

questions.explanation: Layer-wise learning rates assign different learning rates to parameters in different circuit layers. Typically, later layers get higher LR since they have more local effects.

questions.question: Which are valid learning rate scheduling strategies? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Step decay (reduce by factor periodically), exponential decay (continuous exponential reduction), and cosine annealing (cosine-shaped decay) are all valid strategies. Random walk is not a proper scheduling method.
